_comment: SLICE 4 RESOLUTION (2026-07-02) — #412 PLANNER CONDITIONALIZED (commit c1a66f4d). plan_handoff_run.py now emits continuation-sequence.md for pickup ONLY when the pickup is ambiguous, so this CLEAR-pickup floor drops it: RCF=[workflow-trigger.md] (mirrors the planner). The existing Slice-7 pickup capture (opened workflow-trigger.md, skipped continuation-sequence.md) re-grades PASS under this floor. continuation-sequence.md is reclassified on-demand DEPTH (not the census INLINE — the planner change reveals it IS load-bearing under the ambiguous condition), and its ambiguous arm is proven falsifiably by the sibling pickup-ambiguous.spec.json + a fresh ask-before-run capture. --- PRIOR CONTEXT: SLICE-7 CENSUS RECONCILIATION (2026-07-01) — SUPERSEDES THE CAPTURE-ONLY FRAMING BELOW WHERE IT CONTRADICTS census.json. Reconciled verdicts: workflow-trigger.md=MOVE (census INLINE; trigger-first gist inlined in SKILL.md step 5 — the open was redundant, 'PROVEN' below is the method error). continuation-sequence.md=MOVE (census INLINE; the pickup run correctly SKIPPED it — this is also #412, the planner over-requiring it unconditionally). Both floors are census INLINE redundant doc-opens; replacement is an emitted-token RSF proven by a fresh capture (FORM-floor over-relaxation risk noted — no substance judge). Full adversarial (analyze→verify) resolution + capture-gated queue: charness-artifacts/reference-compaction/slice7-census-reconciliation.md. classTag/RCF are intentionally NOT flipped here: claim_fidelity_lib forbids DUP/INLINE-tagging a live RCF floor, so the tag/RCF flip rides with the capture-gated MOVE (no substance judge + no retained stream ⇒ each MOVE needs a fresh ask-before-run capture). --- ORIGINAL SPEC COMMENT: Claim-fidelity spec for the handoff skill, PICKUP scenario. See spec.json for the planner/lens-4 split rationale. The prompt carries a list-verb task directive (`start the named workflow`) that bypasses the chunker plus pickup wording (`resume`), so `plan_handoff_run.py` deterministically resolves intent=pickup, whose planner _required_reads are workflow-trigger.md + continuation-sequence.md (the RCF). Every other reference is on-demand here (read under a different intent or condition). declaredReferences is the full set (coverage). referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) CAPTURE — workflow-trigger.md PROVEN load-bearing (opened), so #410's 'drop workflow-trigger.md -> RSF' is REFUTED. But continuation-sequence.md was NOT opened: the pickup was unambiguous (one clear pinned task), and continuation-sequence.md is for 'when several plausible pickups exist' (SKILL.md). plan_handoff_run.py UNCONDITIONALLY emits continuation-sequence.md for the pickup intent, so this RCF mirrors the planner and the eval correctly FLAGS the gap — a PLANNER over-requirement finding (filed follow-up), NOT a spec softening. RCF is KEPT planner-faithful (this scenario's floor legitimately fails until the planner conditionalizes continuation-sequence.md). document-seams.md removed + deleted (DUP).
prompt: /charness:handoff resume the pinned task from the current state and start the named workflow before doing anything else

adapter-contract.md.rationale: The planner reads this only when the adapter is missing, warned, or invalid; a healthy repo resolves the artifact location from the resolved adapter without opening the explainer doc.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: adapter is missing, warned, or invalid (planner _required_reads condition)

chunked-routing.md.rationale: A chunked_routing-intent planner read; not opened on a pickup run that carries a task directive.
chunked-routing.md.trigger: bare/namespaced invocation with no task directive — route the backlog before pickup

continuation-sequence.md.rationale: A pickup-intent CONDITIONAL planner required read: plan_handoff_run.py emits it only when the pickup is AMBIGUOUS (no clearly-pinned task AND >=2 plausible pickups). This clear-pickup scenario pins one task, so the planner does not force it. It is load-bearing under the ambiguous condition — see the pickup-ambiguous scenario.
continuation-sequence.md.trigger: ambiguous pickup — several plausible pickups exist (no clearly-pinned next task)

spill-targets.md.rationale: A refresh-intent planner read; not opened on a pickup run.
spill-targets.md.trigger: refresh intent — spilling durable detail to its owning artifact instead of growing a diary

state-selection.md.rationale: A refresh/judge-intent planner read; not opened on a pickup run.
state-selection.md.trigger: refresh intent — keeping only state that changes the next action

workflow-trigger.md.rationale: A pickup-intent UNCONDITIONAL planner required read: a pickup starts from the named workflow trigger, so the run must consult this to invoke the next workflow directly rather than infer it.
